issues.body: The new `SPARQ_ZK_REQUIRE_TOOLCHAIN` switch correctly turns a missing nargo/bb installation into failure when explicitly set, but no CI configuration in the diff sets it. Consequently default test runs still return normally when the tools are absent, and the real `CircuitProver -> capture` seam remains optional coverage rather than a continuously enforced contract.
issues.fix_hint: Add or update a toolchain-equipped CI lane to run this test with `SPARQ_ZK_REQUIRE_TOOLCHAIN=1`, so absence of the tools or breakage in bb's emitted artifacts cannot silently pass.
issues.title: Required-toolchain mode is not exercised by CI in this change

verdict.summary: Round 2 resolves the prior major compatibility defect: the TypeScript consumer, shipped fixture, generator, and Rust serializer now consistently use the plural `subProofs` schema, and the site fixture test exercises the same age-gate selection used by the real consumer. The ZK claims remain explicitly research-grade and pending external audit. The native prove/capture test now supports a fail-required mode, but this diff does not wire that mode into a toolchain-equipped CI lane, so ordinary runs can still report success without exercising the native seam. That residual coverage issue is minor and does not invalidate the serialization change.
